target: Section 3.1 (Live Power Dashboard) Domain #20 + Bottom Line, and Section 3.2 (Eclipse) FSF reference corrections, in data/sections.json part3
status_note: SEC-3.2 was already substantially rewritten between the curmudgeon Cycle 1 review and now — it now addresses E-PRED tests, dual baselines, and FSF correctly. The original issues (ISS-082/083/084) are MOSTLY resolved in the current text. This expansion provides TARGETED PATCHES for remaining gaps rather than a full rewrite. Note: EXP-004.json already contained SEC-4.8 content from a previous run (ISS-418/ISS-419); that content belongs in EXP-009 and should be preserved.

patches.target: SEC-3.1 Domain #20 table row in data/sections.json part3.html
patches.description: Domain #20 in SEC-3.1 still shows stale dash-17 to dash-21 nT range. Must be updated to reflect the dual-baseline system described in SEC-3.2.
patches.find: 20Eclipse 2026Pending. Dome range (−17 to −21 nT) overlaps the Chapman-mechanism (when the Moon’s shadow shuts off sunlight, the upper atmosphere loses its electrical conductivity, disrupting magnetic currents at ground level) range (5–20 nT). Stated globe prediction of “0.0 nT” is a straw man. See Section 3.2.
patches.replace_with: 20Eclipse 2026Pending. Dome registers dual baselines: BOU (−5 to −13 nT) and W004 (−10 to −26 nT) across 9 INTERMAGNET stations, spanning nearly the entire plausible quiet-day Chapman-mechanism range (5–25 nT). The BOU baseline predictions fall entirely within standard physics expectations. See Section 3.2 for full analysis including the four E-PRED sub-tests.

patches.target: SEC-3.1 Bottom Line paragraph in data/sections.json part3.html
patches.description: Bottom line states Polaris is 'contradicted by data' but Domain #17 table says the measurement 'doesn't discriminate.' These are directly contradictory. Fix to be consistent with table while preserving the dome's OPEN-006 self-contradiction.
patches.find: Of the remaining 6, the globe predicts the same or better in 5, and the dome is contradicted by data in 1 domain (Polaris excess, where the dome model’s own refraction formula fails by two orders of magnitude).
patches.replace_with: Of the remaining 6, the globe predicts the same or better in 5, and the 6th (Polaris) fails to discriminate — the claimed +0.27° excess falls within the error budget of atmospheric refraction, polar offset, and instrument precision, while the dome’s own OPEN-006 concedes systematic altitude excesses of +0.32° to +1.29° across latitudes (disagreeing with the +0.27° claimed here by up to a factor of 5).

patches.target: SEC-3.2 E-PRED-B paragraph in data/sections.json part3.html
patches.description: The text says 'the FSF values (0.642-2.075) differ from the single factor 1.672 that appears in the dome's monitor.py codebase'. But 1.672 is approximately 10.9/6.5 = KAPPA, the magnetic-gravity coupling constant (WIN-012), NOT an eclipse FSF value. monitor.py's KAPPA is a completely unrelated quantity in a different physical context. This conflation makes us look like we don't understand the model. Replace with accurate observation about FSF non-derivation.
patches.find: We note that the FSF values in the dome’s predictions page (0.642–2.075) differ from the single factor 1.672 that appears in the dome’s monitor.py codebase, suggesting the “geometric derivation” may not be consistently implemented.
patches.replace_with: We note that the FSF values on the dome’s predictions page (0.642–2.075) are presented without derivation — the page states they come from “V12 H(r)/r dome geometry” but the calculation is not shown. Since H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619), the ratio H(r)/r should be deterministically computable from each station’s radial position on the disc, yet the dome does not publish the station radial coordinates used. Without verifiable inputs, the per-station FSF values function as free parameters fitted to produce plausible predictions, not geometric derivations.

patches.target: SEC-3.2 'Structural problems' point 1 in data/sections.json part3.html
patches.description: Same KAPPA/FSF conflation in the 'No dome geometry enters the prediction' paragraph. The text says monitor.py 'uses a different value (1.672) than the per-station FSF values on the predictions page.' This is incorrect -- 1.672 in monitor.py is KAPPA (10.9/6.5), not an FSF value.
patches.find: Even the FSF factors, claimed to derive from “V12 H(r)/r dome geometry,” are not independently verifiable — the computation is not shown on the dome’s predictions page, and the dome repository’s monitor.py uses a different value (1.672) than the per-station FSF values on the predictions page.
patches.replace_with: Even the FSF factors, claimed to derive from “V12 H(r)/r dome geometry,” are not independently verifiable — the computation is not shown on the dome’s predictions page, and the dome’s monitor.py does not appear to implement per-station FSF checking for the eclipse prediction at all.

holes_addressed: ISS-074: Live Power page not archived — DEFERRED. Cannot archive externally in this run. Noted that SEC-3.1's domain classifications rely on unarchived source. Recommend poller extract the Live Power page on next enhancement pass.
ISS-082: Stale '−17 to −21 nT' range — FIXED in patch P1 (Domain #20 table row). Note: SEC-3.2 body was already updated to reflect dual-baseline system in a previous patch run.
ISS-083: E-PRED-B ignored — ALREADY FIXED in current SEC-3.2 text (full paragraph on E-PRED-B with standard physics factors that could produce Hartland > Ebro). No further action needed.

ISS-084: 1.672 KAPPA conflated with FSF — FIXED in patches P3 and P4. Replaced misleading monitor.py KAPPA comparison with accurate observation about FSF non-derivation and lack of monitor.py eclipse FSF checking.

Curmudgeon SEC-3.1 hole 1 (Polaris bottom-line vs table inconsistency) — FIXED in patch P2. Bottom line now consistent with Domain #17 table entry. Preserves the OPEN-006 self-contradiction argument without overclaiming.

Curmudgeon SEC-3.1 hole 3 (fitted constant framing imprecise) — ALREADY FIXED in current text. The H(r) curve-fitting explanation (three data points, two free parameters) is already in the opening paragraph.
Curmudgeon SEC-3.1 hole 4 (statistical independence oversimplified) — ALREADY FIXED in current text. The G/c/h-bar analogy and out-of-sample prediction argument are already present.

Curmudgeon SEC-3.1 stronger argument (code_analysis cross-reference) — ALREADY PRESENT in current bottom-line paragraph.
still_deferred: ISS-074: Archiving the Live Power page requires web fetch capability or poller enhancement. Cannot do in analyst run.
Cross-validation test proposal (curmudgeon SEC-3.1 stronger argument 1) — would strengthen the tautology argument but requires careful statistical framing. Could be a future enhancement.
Effective degrees of freedom calculation (curmudgeon SEC-3.1 stronger argument 2) — back-of-envelope sigma recalculation with ~7 effective tests. Could be added but needs care to avoid overstatement.
kernel_of_truth: The dome model's eclipse prediction suite is genuinely the most scientifically structured element of the entire model. The per-station specificity, dual baselines, four discriminating sub-tests, and blockchain pre-registration represent a real attempt at falsifiable prediction-making. E-PRED-B (Hartland > Ebro despite lower coverage) is a genuine empirical question that standard physics doesn't trivially resolve — local ionospheric conductivity, Sq current geometry, and station latitude all modulate eclipse magnetic signals in ways that make the station ordering non-obvious. The dome's ambition here is commendable even while the hedging structure undermines it. Similarly, the Live Power dashboard's aspiration to multi-domain convergence mirrors genuine scientific methodology — the problem is the shared fitted parameter, not the ambition.
anticipated_objections: Dome: 'FSF IS derivable — H(r)/r at each station's radial position.' → Counter: publish the station radial coordinates and show the calculation. Until then, the FSF values are assertions, not derivations. We would update our text immediately if the derivation were published.

Dome: 'The dual baselines reflect genuine calibration uncertainty, not hedging.' → Counter: calibration uncertainty that spans a factor of 2 (BOU vs W004) and covers nearly the entire plausible signal space is methodologically equivalent to hedging, regardless of intent. The W004 baseline's provenance (failed weekly test relabeled as empirical baseline) compounds this concern.

Dome: 'You can't verify our domain classifications without the Live Power page.' → Counter: valid criticism. We acknowledge this gap (ISS-074). The structural argument (shared lambda_g destroys independence) holds regardless of the specific page layout, because the mathematical dependence on lambda_g is visible in the dome's published model equations.

verification_notes.confirmed_dual_baseline_data: From raw-text/03-predictions.txt: BOU baseline (-10.9 nT), W004 baseline (-22.24 nT). Per-station predictions verified: BOU range -5.0 (SNK) to -12.8 (HAD), W004 range -10.3 (SNK) to -26.2 (HAD). All numbers match the current SEC-3.2 text.
verification_notes.confirmed_kappa_identity: From raw-text/01-home.txt line 32: 'Coupling constant: 1.67 nT/uGal (WIN-012)'. From raw-text/02-wins.txt line 87: 'coupling = 10.9 nT / 6.5 uGal = 1.67 nT/uGal'. The value 1.672 in our text = 10.9/6.5 = 1.6769... rounded. This IS KAPPA, NOT an eclipse FSF value. Confirmed the curmudgeon's ISS-084 finding.
verification_notes.confirmed_sec32_already_fixed: The current SEC-3.2 text includes: (1) dual-baseline description with per-station predictions, (2) all four E-PRED analyses, (3) correct FSF terminology, (4) W004 provenance critique, (5) hedging structure analysis, (6) acknowledgment that quiet-condition filtering is standard practice. ISS-082 and ISS-083 core concerns are resolved. Only the two 1.672 references (P3, P4) and the SEC-3.1 cross-references (P1, P2) remain.
verification_notes.polaris_consistency_check: Domain #17 table: 'Neither model is tested by this; the measurement isn't precise enough to discriminate.' Bottom line (old): 'dome is contradicted by data in 1 (Polaris)'. These are directly contradictory. Patch P2 aligns bottom line with table. The OPEN-006 reference is preserved as a separate point (dome's own data contradicts its own claim, independent of our assessment).
